displayTitle: Walk the Signal Back Up the Chain

beats.narration: Level One qualified you to run the test. Level Two makes you sign for what an indication is, so every signal must be defensible.

payload.title: EXPLAIN, THEN SIGN
payload.sub: Level II: defend every callout from theory.

beats.narration: The method is one five-link chain. Coil current builds a field, the part answers with eddy currents and an opposing field, and impedance carries the message.

payload.heading: Walk the signal back up the chain
payload.note: CURRENT → FIELD → EDDIES → OPPOSING FIELD → IMPEDANCE

beats.narration: The dot only ever shows link five. Nonmagnetic conductor: reactance falls, resistance rises. Ferromagnetic part: permeability wins and reactance climbs instead.

payload.heading: Reading link five: the impedance move

beats.narration: Now the depth story. Current decays exponentially with depth. One standard depth leaves thirty-seven percent; three standard depths, just five, the working limit.

payload.heading: Skin effect: one curve, three numbers

beats.narration: Two formulas solve every problem this course throws: delta near six-sixty over the root of frequency, permeability, and conductivity; phase lag, fifty-seven degrees per depth.

payload.sub: Frequency is the only term you control.
